title: calycobolus gilgianus
intro: Calycobolus gilgianus, also known as Gilg's calycobolus, is a type of flowering plant in the family Convolvulaceae. The plant is traditionally used in folk medicine, particularly in Africa, due to its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiviral activities. The health benefits of Calycobolus gilgianus include its potential to treat various ailments such as fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, as well as its ability to aid in wound healing. In traditional medicine, the plant is often prepared as a tea or infusion, and its leaves and roots are used in herbal preparations to harness its medicinal properties.
